rulings.Case Title: PURE ENTERTAINMENT LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. SHUI KING KWON HOM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Award Of Supplemental Attorneys Fees And Costs Following Appeal; Request For Entry Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 08, 2022, line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT DENNIS PETROU, BRIAN UTTER's Motion For An Award Of Supplemental Attorneys Fees And Costs Following Appeal; Request For Entry Of Judgment. GRANTED as to the requested $78,916.50 fees for appellate work. Cross-defendants provide sufficient justification that the requested fees are reasonable. Hearing required to address why cross-defendants are entitled to an additional $20,120.00 of fees for trial court work performed between February 14, 2020, when Lenders filed their original Motion for Attorneys' fees, and Hom's Notice of Appeal on November 12, 2020. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION VS. DALIA VILCHEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 08, 2022, line 3. DEFENDANT DALIA VILCHEZ's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. GRANTED. No opposition filed. This Court finds that the default was entered due to excusable neglect pursuant to CCP 473(b). Defendant to file Answer forthwith. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
